explanatory_strength.rationale: Four quantified thesis pillars tie ETF and institutional fee stacks, AUM scale (¥1.34tn), operating leverage (FY2026 ROE ~59%), and mid-cycle owner-cash normalization to a croupier mechanism. Structural versus cyclical labeling is explicit (FY2025 ¥137.14/sh anchor vs FY2026 peak ¥317.33). The competitive-advantage case is only partial: filings disclose a single GAAP segment, global ETF sponsors compete on product, and the mispricing story conflicts across ¥464 last print versus ¥757 Yahoo close used in valuation.json.

evidence_sufficiency.rationale: FY2026 finfo2026 and Pnotice2026-1 reconcile cleanly in filing_facts_2026-07-24.json; contract calculation proofs are 100% valid with four non-overlapping components. Material gaps remain for Hohn fieldwork: no yuho/EDINET segment detail, management_facts are boilerplate and unverified, no broker-confirmed live quote or depth, and the price input (Yahoo ¥757) is not reconciled to the documented illiquid last print (¥464, 100 shares).

downside_control.rationale: performance_fee_cycle_reserve (−¥140/sh base) sizes cyclical tail risk and net_financial_claims adds ¥65/sh book cross-check, but downside is not execution-controlled: public feeds show almost no TSE volume, there is no dividend floor, and owner cash is highly market-beta with success fees at ~65% of combined fees in FY2026. A normalized earnings step-down toward FY2025 levels is plausible without a contractual or structural floor.

return_vs_alternatives.rationale: Contract base annualized return at ¥757 is 16.94% (low 6.49%, high 26.83%) on ¥2,264/sh additive value versus spot, marginally clearing a ~15% hurdle on paper. Lawrence synthesis 38.04% overweight bull scenario envelopes and is not the audited component proof. Realized return versus alternatives is unknowable until a live, size-able quote exists; upside/downside asymmetry cannot be tested at a 100-share last print.

claims.claim: FY2026 success fees were ¥14.316B (+53.6% YoY) versus base fees ¥7.869B (+17.1%) on AUM ¥1.3357tn (+2.9%), so performance fees were ~65% of combined fees while the Lawrence anchor uses FY2025 mid-cycle owner cash ¥137.14/sh, not peak FY2026 ¥317.33/sh.

Universal contract sums four additive components to ¥2,264.38/sh base (core_fee_engine ¥2,239, etf_product_pipeline ¥100, net_financial_claims ¥65, performance_fee_cycle_reserve −¥140) with valid calculation proofs and no overlap double-count.

Public market data show a non-continuous TSE tape (two volume days in twelve months; last documented print ¥464 for 100 shares in January 2026), so modeled returns at Yahoo ¥757 are not an investable entry without broker confirmation.

strongest_counter_explanation: The market is rationally ignoring an illiquid micro-float: even filing-strong fee economics and a deep discount to mid-cycle owner cash cannot be harvested because there is no continuous price or liquidity, and FY2026 earnings power is inflated by cyclical success fees that will mean-revert without a durable franchise moat versus global ETF sponsors.
most_important_missing_fact: Broker- or exchange-verified live TSE quote, bid-ask depth, and average daily volume post 1-for-20 split, to test whether ¥757 (Yahoo 2026-07-23) or ¥464 (last 100-share print) reflects an executable entry price.
falsifiers: Two consecutive quarters with combined base+success fees falling more than 25% YoY while Japan equity AUM is flat, without offsetting base-fee rate increases
Operating margin compresses below 35% on revenue while success-fee share stays above 50%, indicating fee compression rather than pure market cyclicality
Confirmed average daily volume remains below ¥50mn notional for six months, proving the discount is a permanent liquidity trap rather than a mispriced franchise
specialist_findings: Competitive-advantage mechanics are mapped as a Japan asset-manager and ETF croupier: fee stack on AUM, high ROE, partial moat from niche listed products, and explicit cyclical performance-fee reserve. Overlap controls cleanly separate core_fee_engine, etf_product_pipeline, net_financial_claims, and performance_fee_cycle_reserve with component_sum_reconciles and no_double_counting both true. Audit concerns: (1) core_fee_engine DCF uses judgment growth (2%) and 12× exit on mid-cycle cash while Lawrence scenarios use 5%/3.5% and 16×, creating parallel return paths; (2) etf_product_pipeline ¥100/sh base is bounded judgment without incremental launch ROIC ledger; (3) price today conflicts between illiquid ¥464 narrative and ¥757 valuation input; (4) no management fieldwork and yuho segment economics absent. Hohn power zone fit is valid (croupier, financial_services; partial dhando not excluded).
